comment: Stage H.1 — applet supply chain: pinned SHA-256 hashes of every CAP
card-ops will install on a chip.  cap-loader.ts hashes the loaded
bytes at install time and refuses any CAP whose digest doesn't match.
Update protocol when a new CAP is dropped in cap-files/:
2. Update the entry below (sha256 + version + builtAt + builtBy)
3. Commit the new CAP and the manifest in the same commit
Future hardening (deferred): cosign signing of CAPs alongside Docker
images.  Adds a `cosignSig` field per entry verified at the same
checkpoint as the hash.  Tracked separately because the applet build
runs off the deploy pipeline today (no GHA cosign integration yet).
An entry whose `sha256` field is empty disables the check for that
key (back-compat for new entries before we've pinned).  A FILE that
exists in cap-files/ but has no manifest entry is REJECTED at
loadCap() time — silent unpinned CAPs are exactly the supply-chain

pa-v3.builtBy: operator (off-pipeline ant build); pre-dates today's C16/C23 + cleanup commits.  Does NOT match the artefact currently on the trial card (which was installed via gp.jar directly during today's session).  Refresh this file + bump sha256 next time card-ops installs pa-v3 via the WS path.

t4t.builtBy: PalisadeT4T.cap not yet checked in to cap-files/; loadCap('t4t') returns CapFileMissing today.

receiver.builtBy: test-receiver.cap not yet checked in to cap-files/; loadCap('receiver') returns CapFileMissing today.
